name: Embalse de Alarcón – circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón
name_original: Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Olmedilla de Alarcón
region_intro: Hiking around Olmedilla De Alarcón offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by the presence of the Júcar River and the Embalse de Alarcón reservoir. The region features a mix of open landscapes and areas with historical structures like the Castle of Alarcón. Elevation changes are present, providing diverse hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Olmedilla De Alarcón The most popular hiking route is Hoz de Alarcón Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features varied terrain and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Embalse de Alarcón – circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Castle of Alarcón – Beautiful view of the Júcar River loop from Alarcón, a 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through historic areas, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Olmedilla De Alarcón is defined by routes along the Júcar River, views of the Embalse de Alarcón, and paths near historic castles. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Olmedilla De Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Olmedilla De Alarcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: There are around 8 hiking routes in the Olmedilla De Alarcón area, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: Yes, the region offers a couple of easy routes. While specific easy routes are not detailed in the provided data, the overall selection includes options suitable for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: What are some good options for a moderate hike in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: For a moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013914793/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the reservoir. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alarcón – Beautiful view of the Júcar River loop from Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, leading through historic areas.

Q: Are there any challenging trails for experienced hikers in the area?
A: Yes, for a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013191425/hoz-de-alarcon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Alarcón Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route featuring varied terrain and significant elevation changes, perfect for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular walks around Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013914793/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails often feature the Júcar River and the Embalse de Alarcón reservoir. You can also encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through the Gorges of Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alarcón – Alarcón Viewpoint loop from Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead you to scenic vistas. You can also find beautiful views of the Júcar River from various points, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful view of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt; highlight.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: The trails in Olmedilla De Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the reservoir and river, and the historical points of interest along the routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed, Olmedilla De Alarcón and nearby Alarcón are generally accessible by car. It is advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for parking options near your chosen trailhead.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Olmedilla De Alarcón suitable for families?
A: The region offers a mix of difficulties, so families can find suitable options. While some routes are challenging, there are also moderate and easy trails that could be enjoyable for families, depending on the children's age and experience. Look for shorter, less strenuous routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: The region can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winters are typically mild, allowing for hiking, though some trails might be less accessible depending on conditions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Olmedilla De Alarcón?
A: Many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially in natural areas. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions. Always carry water for your dog.
tips:
The Alarcón dam is a hydroelectric engineering work built on the upper course of the Júcar river. It is located 6 km from the town of Alarcón, in the province of Cuenca.
Its construction began in 1942 at the initiative of the Valencian irrigators to regulate the course of the river, essential for the irrigation of the province of Valencia, finishing the works in 1970.

Quiet town in the interior of Cuenca but with several services and very well located to access the Alarcón reservoir. From here you can follow a path along the shore of the reservoir, very picturesque and ideal for the summer months when you can take advantage of a refreshing bath.
